programmes.show_description: Featuring performances from Gruff Rhys, Ye Vagabonds, KhakiKid, RÓIS, Melin Melyn , Panic Shack, Sorry & Master Peace.
Tim Renkow welcomes comedians Janine Harouni and Stuart Goldsmith to the stage.
Programmes start at 12:05.
Tane and Jo are enjoying the early-days bliss of their new relationship, happy to finally be together after what felt like a lifetime of longing.
John has a cuppa with Dana and checks in on how she's going post-breakup. She tells him she's just trying to keep busy and has taken on some extra shifts at work.
Sonny's changed his emergency contact to Remi after breaking up with Dana, but Remi had no idea.
After female mechanic Jess reluctantly agrees to participate in a car restoration show, she is shocked to learn that her ex-boyfriend, Luke, is her main competitor.
It's the final, and after challenges including opera gloves, party paraphernalia and a draped evening outfit, one sewer will be crowned winner.
It's up to the wire as the planners battle to impress the judges and pull off a millionaire's garden party for Lauren and Jodie.
Sparks fly as the planners clash over plans for a multi-themed wedding, their toughest challenge yet. With two venues to arrange, can they pull together and impress the judges?
The latest news and updates from the UEFA Champions League.

programmes.show_name: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond The Lobby
programmes.show_description: Rob Rinder and Monica Galetti work 3000 meters above sea-level at Gangtey Lodge, Bhutan, in a remote region that only obtained electricity 11 years ago.
In Eastbourne, a routine day at the station changes in an instant when a boat catches fire just outside the harbour.
The Nazi Child Army: Part one of two. The story of the Nazi youth organisation, which was used to indoctrinate German boys in the 1930s and prepare them for the war Hitler was planning.
Fact-based thriller, recreating the 1944 plot by a conspiracy of rebel German officers to assassinate Adolf Hitler using a briefcase bomb. Starring Tom Cruise and Kenneth Branagh.
As Abby attempts to bring the train to a halt, the remaining passengers turn on Joe.
Claudia greets 22 strangers as they arrive at a beautiful castle in the Scottish Highlands to play the ultimate reality game of detection, backstabbing and trust.
Programmes Start at 12:15.
A journalist faces old fears when she returns to her hometown ice rink to cover a story. Romantic drama, starring Celeste Desjardins and Marcus Rosner.
The day that James and Helen have been dreading arrives. Mrs Hall takes a step forward with Gerald. Carmody finds his place at Skeldale.
Helen decides to move back with her father while James is away. Siegfried is frustrated by a constant leaking tap in the kitchen.
Wildlife documentary following the animals that stroll through Paris in search of food, love and adventure while people go about their daily lives.
Kalahari: Simon Reeve crosses the parched desert of southern Africa, tracking wildebeest with the famous hunter-gatherers of the Kalahari, before heading to the oasis of the Okavango Delta.

programmes.show_name: Serengeti: Journey to the Heart of Africa
programmes.show_description: Life happened in the Serengeti, where nature has orchestrated an ecosystem in which every species has a distinct role to play.

programmes.show_name: Russia's Revenant - The Return of the Siberian Tiger
programmes.show_description: A giant Siberian tiger's journey to find a new territory, following the big cat as he navigates forests and encounters humans.
Two world-leading projects designed to tackle the crises facing Earth's rivers - one in the American West, the other in Paris.
Cigarettes and Alcohol and Rollerblading: The priest tries to give up cigarettes for Lent, inspired by a challenge from his opposite number over on Rugged Island.
Joanne Cantwell is joined by Ciaran Whelan and Lee Keegan for highlights and analysis of the Football League Division 1 & 2 finals.
